item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1, buuuuut I think that we have too little information on 1864-3, we basically know that they can't be recorded, were created/betrayed by -2 and want to free him.

The tale adds info on -2 capabilities but subtracts mistery somehow in the wrong way (I'm clearly commenting that entry-wise)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: o7 Thank you as well, Kaktus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Elogee brought it to my attention that the old images were no longer compliant. Thus, new images. Image credits are in the header bar.

Thanks, licensing stalin!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Initially, I liked this. It almost felt like 1730 - the mysterious location, the dangerous, winding pathways, the words on the walls, and what I hoped was an entertaining exploration log.

The imagery is great, for sure. I can imagine the dark maze, the big pool, and the grate (or more accurately, the window to the prison cell). 1864-1's description is a little confusing - I'm imagining a tall monolith-like torso dragging itself across the ground with an arm - but it fits well with the setting.

1864-3 is very disappointing though. We get the bare minimal description of "Non-Humanoid" and the weak excuse of "it can't be perceived" (apparently, the Foundation didn't try anything beyond cross-examining witness reports). There's nothing to visualize other than a formless, faceless monster that we have to assume ignores humans and attacks 1864-1… somehow. Maybe it eats people. Maybe it shoots lasers from its tentacles. Maybe it does nothing and just dies.

The exploration log barely helped. The gratuitous use of *Silence* at the beginning is fitting because it represents the amount of information we're provided. The agents, when encountering 1864-3, don't say anything useful for the camera (we don't even get some conflicting descriptions). The log ends at 1864-1 for unknown reasons, we don't know what happens to the agents, and the events during first contact are unknown. This wouldn't have been so bad if there was the briefest explanation of [REDACTED] or "the agents all died".

Most SCPs are supposed to leave us curious and wanting more. That's fair. But this doesn't make me curious, it makes me frustrated by the lack of substance and the Foundation's apparent failure at anything resembling adequate documentation. The only question I'm left with is, What was the point of all this?

-1

item.title: STAFF POST - OPEN Crosslink proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

show reasoning
hide reasoning

Due to the very tight narrative of this article, cross-linking /anything/ at all was tough to do. As other mainlist articles within the resurrection canon have linked their respective tales, the wikiwalk team has come to the conclusion that it would be appropriate to link this article to its respective tale.

Editing this:

Berlin when the next ship arrives, and we will secure the glorious victory for the Fatherland. This boy Pan Hun will be our salvation!

To this:

Berlin when the next ship arrives, and we will secure the glorious victory for the Fatherland. This boy Pan Hun will be our salvation!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tentatively, I feel that Dr Groswalds' journal doesn't add any new information, just repeats it, perhaps a little more clearly, but no more dramatically, while making the Doctor/pool attendant overly familiar. Frankly, I'd remove it.

Also, the islands' behavior of disappearing for most of the month has some potential for drama (get out before your trapped here with *them* for a month), but isn't really played out in a satisfying way, so it just gives the reader an extra anomaly to be distracted by, and another code-word to deal with, instead of getting right into the juicy flesh of the article.

Other than that, sets a nice creepy scene, makes one imagine wandering that strange labyrinth, and feel for that strange, demented guardians' plight. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall I liked this one. However

It is currently believed that this entity, designated SCP-1864-2, is [DATA EXPUNGED]

and

Through discussions with SCP-1864-1, it appears as if these entities are attempting to reach SCP-1864-2, which would result [DATA EXPUNGED]

seem unnecessary. If you don't want us to know what -2 is exactly that's fine, but instead of just putting a sheet over the thing and saying "don't look" just make it so that the Foundation doesn't know either. Would make it feel actually mysterious and less lazy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could very well be wrong, but i thought this was kinda like the locket. Like he has healing powers, but they're too good and end up turning the people into eldritch stuff. he's the messenger of god alright, but its some cthulu horror god and not the one theyre thinking of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Um, kaktus? I can infer it, I think, but you never say what the materialization events are. I'm assuming it's either the new moon, when the -3s appear, or both, but if it's both (which would make the most sense I think), then why's it worded like the -3s appear randomly?

You get my +1, but that just leaves me scratching my head trying to figure out what the new moon has to do with anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bizarre, chilling, leaves just enough left unsaid. Amazing work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seeing as how he's called the lonely liar, I'd say his intentions were never in these people's best interests.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved it but would anyone mind explaining this

Pan Hun found> Turns out he is gods messenger> ??? > Now he's all evil or sumthing? I guess i didnt quite get the gap of what causes him to go bad or if he was bad from the start or what. None the less I enjoyed it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm getting that feeling that this is referencing something that I don't know…. +1 regardless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I almost always like SCPs that demonstrate that the Foundation is not the only organization/people that have tried to contain anomalies/threats. This doctor did, and is continuing to do, whatever he can to prevent Pan Hun from escaping and becoming a global threat. My hat is off to him for his sacrifice and dedication.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1864-1 is a Class II Near-Humanoid entity, standing at a height of roughly 4.3m. SCP-1864-1 has an elongated torso that connects to the floor beneath it,

1) Does it merge with the ground at the waist, at the hip, or somewhere else?

2) Is it 4.3m from floor to head, or is that how tall it would be if it still had legs?

These entities move erratically, cannot be captured by video or audio monitoring equipment, and seem able to alter an individual's memory of how they are perceived.

How do they know they're altering memories, rather than just appearing different to each person?

To date, all attempts by these entities to reach the grate within the central pool have failed, usually due in major part to hostility towards the entities by SCP-1864-1.

How does he manage to fend them all off, with just a single arm?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent. I almost wish there were more journal entries at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well done. I can hear the frustration and anger in the good doctor's voice. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are correct, and I am feex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: big +1 as well. Really solid scp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very nice work. There's a reference to a new "noon" in the containment procedures which I am 99.99% sure is a typo, but I loathe editing other people's articles.

This is great. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, it's been awhile since I've read such a thorough, creepy, and superbly mysterious SCP. So many unanswered questions, but the imagery was just so vivid and provoking. Well done, kaktus. A big +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm wondering how the doctor managed to contain the boy, considering that he was a normal human before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice, the interview log is what really made this SCP for me though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it a lot! There's a couple things I don't really get, but they're things I'm not really supposed to get, so I'm fine with that. Good job kaktus!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. I want to hug this doctor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I adore how the inscription at the entrance rhymes when translated back into German. That's a sign of quality. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's a good point. I wavered on keeping it in, and it's probably better if it goes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Know what? Not the most original concept ever, but I actually like it, not the least because of the strangely FMA feel it has.
One quibble - the third photo has no real purpose - all it shows are the two agents underwater, and since when were selfies a part of official documentation?

item.title: The Child in the Pit
item.content:encoded:
Considerable thanks to everybody who looked at this in chat, primarily anqxyr, TwistedGears, and a number of others I can't remember right now. Your help is invaluable to me :D

As always, your comments, critiques, and criticisms are always welcome!
